reviews.title: I will never recommend the Sofitel Krabi hotel to any traveler. Do not go. I am so angry with them
reviews.text:
On Jan 4 I was scheduled to stay at this hotel for 2 nights. This is not the first time I stayed here. I stayed here back in 2016 and at the time I thought the hotel was ok.  

I decided to try again and give them another chance.  I booked a room and saved up for almost a year for our vacation. I have a 2 year old who has a condition so I chose this hotel because on the web site it seemed good with kids.

Little did I realize. On Jan 4 Krabi had a storm warning. Largest storm in 30 years. Anyway the hotel was not undertanding of my family. Gave me horrible response and did not appreciate that my little child had a condition. 

I hate this hotel. I will NEVER EVER go there again.
reviews.response: Dear William, thank you for taking your time to share your review. We understand that you are very concerned with the storm warning, however, as we informed you by e-mail that the storm has not impacted during your upcoming period of stay.  Therefore, we hope you understand also that we were unable to modify the booking via third party as per terms and conditions.We sincerely hope that you will give us another opportunity to welcome you again.

reviews.title: Goft resort with disappointment
reviews.text:
We booked room mentioned pool and sea side, while hoping to see stunding seaview but turned out to be very disappointment. If you saw from website picture you will expect small foot steps away from pool but it does not, we have to walk 20 meters to the pool and 1st floor view is suck as this hotel is distance from the beach. I think what hotel means pool and sea side is refer to direction facing not mean few steps from such location, so do take it wrongly. Taking about meaning we should get at least room close to the pool while sea is out reach distance anyway.
Breakfast is expensive as we paid opptionally, less selection but expensive

reviews.title: Acceptable

Nice hotel but doesn’t worth the price. The private beach is in terrible conditions (dirty, no umbrellas) and you must cross a busy road to go there.
I do not recommend it, there are better and nicer hotels at the same price.
reviews.response:
Dear Luca, we regret that the beach in front of the hotel let you down. Normally we are not allowed to install the permanent umbrellas on the public beach. However, we do provide the sunbeds and towels for our Sofitel Guests during the stay. The beach is normally cleaned by our sports team every day. Of course, the conditions can change during the day with the tides, however on most occasions this public beach is very well maintained by us for the enjoyment of our guests

We hope that the rest of your stay was pleasant and we sincerely hope that we will have another opportunity to welcome you again.

They lost my daughter's art work during she was making on kids activity class though They don't agree it's their mistake and kept lying that they gave it us already. Beach also no good compare to beyond and dusit thani in klong muang beach. It was very narrow! I never come back here again!

Your remark has been carefully noted and I can assure you the necessary steps have been taken to avoid such inconveniences in the future. Your feedback is extremely valuable to us as it helps us improve the quality of our service to better meet your needs I hope we will have the pleasure of welcoming you again soon in Krabi.

feeling quite bad actually after staying at the Sheraton 2 minutes far from there. the road on front of the hotel was packed all day with big trucks that you could see from the swimming-pool because of the tiny hedges, and we could inhale the pollution from the swimming-pool very badly; the loud techno music couldn't be avoided whatever the place you were sitting around the pool and the aggressive sun without protection around the pool left us sunburnt and without being able to sleep.
no staff in the gym, no possibility to book any class; very very poor gym equipment (only one machine!) , no staff and no particular food according to my dietary requirements, e.g. gluten-free bread, sugar-free items...

reviews.title: Great location but small rooms
reviews.text:
We arrived at around 4pm. Your staff don't allow us to check in as there are 5 members and we only booked 1 room.The room was booked by my girl and I thought she booked a 2 single bed and all of us can stay in by adding an extra bed. We were than asked to ger another room and without hesitation i agreed but the rate was higher than Agoda's rate. The staff there can't made any decision on every question I asked and she have to keep on referring to her superior on phone. The rate offer are way too high for the second room, therefore I suggest that I have to book thru Agoda again. After the booking my child made a mistake by stating only 1 person is staying and again we have to argue the rate again. After much negotiation we have to pay extra 2000bt  plus but without breakfast even i requested. I commented to the manager there that we are here for holiday and the policies are too strict. The whole scenario have cost us 1hrs plus.
I find that your policies are too tight. I was disappointed that I have to go back to Agoda again  to make another booking  leaving us damsel in distress whereby they can just charge me accordingly on Agoda,s rate?
Neither will I recommend friends. 
The rooms are not fully supply with toiletrie and we have to keep requesting everyday.
Thanks.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Property is expansive and grounds are well maintained.  Especially a good choice for families with kids (even teenagers).  Pool is a bit too big and spread across a large area with kid friendly areas as well but not shaded at all and the pool is not temperature controlled so the water is very hot.  The location is a bit removed and isolated, without a vehicle it is difficult to commute.  It is not a beach property, one has to cross the road to actually get to the beach and it is a public beach so not so well maintained.  We were there during the solar eclipse and the beach was infested with dead jelly fish, which means the water must a lot so need to be especially careful with kids.  Rooms are large and well appointed & comfortable.
reviews.negatives: The resort needs to seriously update their food game.  The breakfast spread is huge but the experience is a bit chaotic, lot of people and uncleared tables and kids screaming so we dreaded to go down everyday.  The menus across the restaurants and the poolside needs an update, it is either fast food or then some run of the mill dishes that is available every other touristy places.  No focus on local ingredients or dishes.  We ordered a chicken with cashew nuts and chicken green curry through room service and it was the worst thai food we have had in thailand and we are huge fans of thai food.  The chicken was grilled in large chunks and just added to a green curry topped with a loooooooot...of baby eggplants and same for the other chicken dish - grilled chicken topped with a sauce - we were extremely disappointed with the selection of thai food in the property.  In fact on the last afternoon of our stay the pool bar did not even have fresh coconuts in stock!!!!

reviews.title: Bad Experience
reviews.text:
Had food poisoning from lunch at the pool bar, even though they sent someone to my room with medicine nobody bothered to check how I was. The lunch was fully charged and I didn't hear an apology from anyone. I paid the mount in full, too exhausted to complain, it wasn't about the money but the expectation you have from a 5-star hotel.

Hotel is right next to a gypsum mine, there was a large vessel loading. No question about swimming in the sea, forget it. Luckily you don't see the vessel loading from the hotel, only from the main road.

They put us on the ground floor, initially it was nice since you could directly walk to the pool from the room, but there was a foul disgusting smell when they were watering the plants, a combination of sewage and manure. Again, complained but nothing, not even an apology. 

Worst vacation ever

reviews.title: The resort looks tired...
reviews.text: The hotel is poorly located. It is not beach front as the pictures might suggest. A road divides the beach and the hotel, and the beach is less than ideal. F&B is disappointing. With only one pricey Italian restaurant available to hotel guests while the all day dining restaurant is often booked for private corporate events. Rent a scooter and escape!
reviews.positives: Clean rooms. Functional gym. Big premise.

reviews.title: A mistake not to be repeated
reviews.text:
The hotel is located across a road from a thin strip of bad beach.  Not attractive.  It is far enough away from any competitive restaurants that the guests have no practical alternatives to the hotel restaurants, which are overpriced and mediocre.

Thai people seem to shun this hotel.  The only Thai I saw worked at the hotel, not guests.  The clientele is almost all Northern European and Australian.  The Euro people claim the pool side lounge chairs by 10 am.  They toss a few towels and personal items on the chairs then go somewhere else.  Maybe they use the chairs during the day, maybe not, but they do exclude others from using them all day.

Don't buy the pool side buffet dinners - don't waste your money.
reviews.positives: Leaving, leaviing, and leaving

reviews.text: The property and the service is very good, however the amenities like the TV, shower extra could be refurbished.

reviews.title: Location are far and rooms are small
reviews.text: we reached sofitel at around 3pm. it took us more than 1 hrs to check in. upon checking in your staff requested us to book another room bcos they don't allow us all to stay in one room. this booking was book by my child and i though she has booked a double single bed room and i thought by adding an extra bed will do. without hesitation i agreed for to take another room but was shocked as they refuse to give me agoda's rate as the rate she offered was too high. i negotiated with her she can't make any decision at all on every request i made, everytime she has to make a phon call on my request. finally i decided to book thru agoda again, she than lead me to the room for booking. again my child made a mistake by clicking 1 perrson staying on 1 room. i thought everything are solved as we booked 2 rooms for 5 person staying. this time a senior japanese manager spoke to us asking us to pay exta 4000bt  plus for 2nd person stay. i told her we come for holiday and her policies are so strick! finally we settle for 2000bt plus and strictly without breakfast knowing is far to travel to town for breakfast. before this i was staying in rachadamri grand central, we book a suite and we were only request to book an extra bed and we were rewarded 5 complementary breakfast and 3 for following days. i will definitely go back to rachadamri to stsy bcos they know how to please costomers but nor sofitel. neither will i recomend any friends.
reviews.positives: Swimming pool

reviews.title: Disappointed by Poor Service and Lack of Safety
reviews.text: Our 3-night stay was far from relaxing. From the start, service was lacking—I requested ice at check-in, followed up multiple times, and only received it nearly 3 hours later after I went to the front desk myself. On the second day, a large corporate group, reportedly from Axis Life Insurance (India), arrived and caused continuous disruption. Their loud, drunken behavior near the children’s pool forced families to leave, and hotel staff seemed powerless to intervene. Breakfast was chaotic due to their shouting and disorderly conduct. Worst of all, on the final night, we were woken by yelling and someone trying to open our door. Management admitted they only had 3 security guards for nearly 300 rooms and couldn’t control the situation. This stay felt unsafe and mismanaged. I have submitted a formal complaint and expect a full apology and compensation.

reviews.title: Not value for money
reviews.text: Overall, very dissapointed with property. While property is big with 2 key attractions - a very big swimming pool and a large golf course. But they have made all their facilities (except pool) as chargeable. On top the room rates are anyways quite high. The beach is also not ideal with small stones in the beach. Even the housekeeping services are very slow. The restaurant opening timings are very limited and 2 out of 3 restaurants open only during evening hours. The in room dining options are very limited. Will not reccommend this property especially considering high price.

reviews.response:

Sawasdee Kha! Thank you very much for staying with us during your recent trip to Krabi and for taking time to share your review on Agoda.com.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: Breakfast was pretty good for a 4* hotel. Unfortunately lacked sea food, however variety is great. If you are eating clean, enough choice to enjoy it
reviews.negatives:
Grossly overpriced for the facilities. Needs major updating in every sense. Aircon is so dirty that I had to drive to a store, buy duck tape and plastic bags to block the dirty dusty air blowing into the room. Even after I blocked it, air still would come thru the cracks around it that made me cough all 7 days non stop from it. Dinner was crap, portions laughably small and everything is fried in oil. Choice on dinner is incredibly limited, had to eat pizza for 7 days straight since everything else was deep fried utter crap and after ordering a crab, I had an empty shell brought to the table. Half board is a scam, offers on the menu specifically excluded half boarders. Price tag very high for it as well.

There’s no beach you can swim, water very murky and muddy. Staff was not very welcoming. I don’t wanna go on since it would get objectively much worse.

I have stayed as much nicer places around for half price.

reviews.title: Not worth the money - overrated
reviews.text: This is a not the standard I expected from a 440$/ night hotel and especially not a Sofitel, it just has the name. When we arrived, the staff at the pool bar/restaurant did not want to serve food because it was apparently too late and the kitchen closed at 5pm but it was 4.45pm and the reception had told us we could eat there. No communication between them and after they finally agreed to serve food they were just unhelpful. Then we wanted to go for a spa treatment but it was apparently fully booked and when we asked again at reception suddenly it was not. Shame that they have those facilities but the service is not there. Rooms are ok but bathrooms are getting quite old. The redeeming feature is the breakfast. Overall I would not go back and neither would I recommend to pay such a premium for this hotel.
reviews.response:
Bonjour 'A',

Thank you for taking your time to share your review. We regret that your recent stay in some areas fell below your expectations. Rest assured that this information has been shared with the respective department heads to follow up with their team. We do apologize for the inconvenience caused. Nevertheless, we are glad that you enjoyed the variety of our breakfast. We will also share your kind feedback with the team. We do hope that you provide us with the opportunity to welcome you back sometime in the future to hopefully restore your faith in Sofitel Krabi.

Yours Sincerely,

Poorit Nookua
Guest Experience Manager - Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf & Spa Resort

reviews.title: Great location, great staff, many hidden costs.
reviews.positives: The Hotels is great when you get there, the staff very friendly and helpful. The Hotel has great facilities, and we really enjoyed our stay at the Hotel. There are a number of F&B options that allows variety and when you stay for an extended time you don't have to eat the same food over and over. The facilities are well maintained and probably as clean as can be expected.
reviews.negatives:
Being in the Hospitality industry myself I understand the challenges and can let many things slide. I know how difficult it is to run a resort however there were a few things to note:
1. Golf: The add stated an 18-hole golf course. it is actually a 9-hole meshy course (par 3 course). We had to rent a set of clubs for myself and my son EACH (was made clear these are the rules, can't share), yet we still had to share because the clubs are incomplete (missing clubs), and they only had female clubs (no metal stiff shaft clubs). All the water holes are empty and under maintenance (this was not indicated during booking). The staff told us how important it is to book since they are very busy in the morning, yet after spending 2 hours on the course from 9am the morning... we were the only people on the course.
2. Meals: We took the half board package but was told that the evening we arrived that there would be no evening buffet anymore. we had to book and use the Italian, Indian or Thia restaurant that you could only select 2 items (for Indian and Thai) and 3 from the Italian menu. Typically, in all other hotels we stayed half board included local alcohol, soft drinks and water, here NOTHING was included, and we had to pay extra for EVERYTHING. Again, not made clear when making the booking.
3. Arrival: It all started on the wrong note when we were left stranded at the airport when nobody showed up from the arrange airport collection. Phoning took 30min only to hear yeh we can't find your airport transfer and was told... use a taxi. Arriving at the Hotel and it was just swept under the carpet.
4. Pool Bar: staff GREAT, service GREAT, they can do with a shade in the pool for the pool bar, there is only a small section that has shading, and everybody huddled in there to get out of the baking sun. Additionally, the poor bartender was dripping of sweat and could do with a few fans in the bar to keep in cool as well.

reviews.title: We arrived at the hotel via taxi as the hotel Does not provide free shuttle service.  The staff were
reviews.text:
The staff were welcoming and warm and assist in our check in promptly. However we were allocate a room at the far end - takes 10 mins to walk to the room. Nevertheless the room was big and nice view. All was alright except that the door cannot be double locked. And worse morning the toilet bowl was choked . So before we go to Ao Nang we made a request at the conceirge to change room
And was informed that was one available
And waiting for the customer to check out. 
After we came back from the town which is after 3pm, we will told that there is no other rooms available imagine the disappointment. 
But the reception lady was kind and search one for me but it’s a superior room and I have booked the luxury room. How can right 
So we waited in the room and after 3 calls they finally confirm that there is one room just 8 doors away from lobby.So happily we moved our luggage and went to the new  room. 
This room at 108 was good and we can just leave from the back door to the pool. Bit
reviews.response: Daer Tan, thank you for taking your time to tell us about your experience at Sofitel Krabi. We regret that you encountered the inconvenience during your recent visit, however, we are glad that the issues were handled already by our Ambassadors. We hope that the rest of your stay was not tainted by the room issue and that you will give us another opportunity to welcome you again in the future.
reviews.room_type: LUXURY ROOM, 1 King Size Bed, Pool and Garden View

reviews.title: Alright…service levels need to improve.
reviews.positives: The property is very huge. Easy access to the beach and restaurants outside. Friendly staff. Breakfast was very good.
reviews.negatives:
Service was very slow. 
The fan was not working in our room and even after informing the room service, no one came to fix it. 
Had asked for an early check in and late check out - neither happened. All 5 star hotels that I have been to do provide some relaxation on check in / check out - but did not happen with Sofitel. In fact this is my first stay at Sofitel.

reviews.title: Bad toilet function
reviews.text:
We stayed 2 nights but the toilet was blocked twice. It has really big problem for the toilet.
Besides, hotel did not provide shaver or refill toiletries, need to call and request

For the used towel, hotel did not take out , still keep in our room

Breakfast is really bad, no choices, taste bad
Staff will not serve you or take away the used plate, staff pour all the used soup in  one bowl in front of us,  it's so disgusting and like eating on street shops.  Service is 2 or 3 star hotel

We won't stay this hotel again
reviews.response: On behalf of Sofitel Krabi Phokeetra, please accept my deepest apologies for the inconvenience you faced during your stay with us. Your remark has been carefully noted and I can assure you the necessary steps have been taken to avoid such inconveniences in the future. Your feedback is extremely valuable to us as it helps us improve the quality of our service to better meet your needs. I hope we will have the pleasure of welcoming you again soon in Krabi.

reviews.title: Place for couples, not for family with todller
reviews.positives: Location, architecture
reviews.negatives: Sold as family friendly with complementary kids club, actually half of activities at the kids club are not free. Scheduled in such a way that parents have to pay…staff at kids club not much involved with kids, hence clubs d always empty as kids are bored.

reviews.title: Ok get away but not so value for money
reviews.text:
The rooms were average for the money we pay. Staff keeps on forgetting to refill amenities and once rolled back used napkin with utensil. We got a junior suite room which was nice but the hotel put us at the furthest part of the hotel. Breakfast was average. The variety of fruit juice was a treat but way too sweet. 
There was a list full of adult and kids activities which I think was pretty good. 
Location wise was a bit far from town; beach not so clean 
Morning trip to Koh Hong disappointing. No sight of clear water or fish; we hired a private long tail boat but driver disappeared and we had to wait a rocky dock for 20 minutes. 
I have to say the staff was friendly the people in Krabi in general is friendly. The pool is ok way too hot needs for shades.
reviews.response: Dear Jenny. we appreciate your positive feedback about our hotel. We take pride in catering to our guests’ needs the best way we can. If you’re traveling to our area again soon, don’t hesitate to contact us.

reviews.title: Good and Bad Experiences
reviews.text:
The bad things about Sofitel in Krabi are:
1. The other Duty Manager (she is a Thai woman) is definitely not a customer service oriented person. She was not helpful and unpleasant to speak with.
2. My wife's rayban was stolen inside her bag in our room when the housekeeping did their job on our first morning at Sofitel. No proper investigation was done nor a follow up. We had to let go of the situation.
3. The beach was not good and should not be considered a beach front hotel.
reviews.positives: The good things about Sofitel in Krabi are its big reception/lobby area, good food at the Indian/ Thai restaurant and very good staff like Annie and Sam - who were genuinely nice and helpful to us.

reviews.title: Tourist
reviews.positives: Swimming Pool, Garden, Travel package

reviews.title: Good for biz trip
reviews.text: Good for biz trip
reviews.positives: Good for biz trip

reviews.title: dont stay during or after a wedding
reviews.text: we booked a pool and sea view room but firstly was put in a side way just seeing a bit of the sea, then after 5 calls and waiting in 4 hours, we managed to shift into another side which this time can only see a bit of the kid pool but no sea. so, it is not a pool and sea view but just pool or sea view. obviously they have some problem with the agreement together agoda,and i bet all the room you booked here has the same problem.  besides, the hotel was not well maintained after 500+ head indian wedding, the staff all looked so tired and so does the facility. so, please call and make sure there is no wedding before or after if you don't want join the big party. actually, heard from the staff it is frequently holding such weddings or conference and personally feel they don't care enough the individual travelers. this area is very rural and nothing to see or eat, unless you would like to eat all day in the hotel and go nowhere but just the huge pool.
reviews.positives: mango by request at breakfast, spacious pool, close to beach (the beach is more like public and just not in great maintainess)

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.positives: friendly staff

reviews.title: Good
reviews.positives: Nice place

reviews.title: Poor management in the hotel
reviews.text: During our stay it was a wedding preparation, so the mangement of the hotel decided to turn off Jacuzzi pool in one part of the hotel and in fact a part of swimming pool was closed also because of a preparation process to wedding and decorating trees, pool and so on. It was very unpleasant for us to be the guests of the hotel and to get such a poor service in these days. Nobody in the hotel said nothing to the guests about future wedding, nobody apologized in advance for any inconvenience this may cause. So, for me it is a very poor service in this hotel. I was in the hotel in 2012 and it was much more better in service that time. Now it is a hotel with a big pool, nothing more.
reviews.positives: check-in and check-out are fast and easy, very big swimming pool

reviews.title: Hard to find the proper taxi
reviews.text: I enjoy staying at Sofitel. However, the transportation from town and other point just hard to get proper taxi.. they use the private car at the taxi to pic up guess at the road site...as a guess we do not know is that secure or not. Hope it ca be improve. Overall stay i rate 5 star.
reviews.positives: Big Swemming pool

reviews.title: Nice hotel no more than that
reviews.positives: Rated as 5 star hotel but it is very basic with medium service dose not worth the money

reviews.title: Great hotel shame about the location
reviews.text:
This is such a lovely hotel but the location is not as good as the Sheraton. You have to cross the road to the beach (although there are staff to help you cross and beach towels). The road is very busy with heavy trucks which makes it dangerous to walk anywhere with small children. We stayed at the Sheraton a few years ago and the beach was part of the hotel and there were shops and restaurants at the hotel entrance. The Sofitel is no better than this but with a worse location where there is a 10-15 minute walk with no pavement along a busy road to the nearest shop and restaurant outside the hotel. 
Having said that, the staff are lovely, the food is nice and the kids club was fun.
reviews.positives: Beautiful pool, good breakfast, nice room

reviews.title: good
reviews.text: good but too far from downtown so taxi was bit expensive
reviews.positives: service, food, view

reviews.title: good
reviews.positives: nice view, huge pool & close to the beach

reviews.title: Lovely Hotel But in the Wrong Location
reviews.text: My husband and I stayed here after Christmas for 3 nights and although the hotel itself was lovely we were very disappointed with its location.  The beach is unusable and across a busy road.  Our rooms were on the top floor and even then we could only get a glimpse of the ocean.  The nearest town is 30 kms away and the pool although large the beds offered no shade.  The umbrellas were held down with 100 kilo ornaments!  It was not at all what I had in mind and I would not recommend it to anybody looking for a beach holiday in Krabi.  There are lots of other hotels around which offer beach access with stunning views of the ocean
reviews.positives: Well Managed Clean Good Breakfast

reviews.title: Nice Hotel and good service
reviews.text:
Hotel per se is very nice and well kept. We had a seminar at the ballroom, wow power failure about 5 times a day. This is very disruptive and unacceptable. Transport is not good, must book in advance.
Van  pickup was disappointing, the driver forget to pick guest and arrived late and guest had to take a taxi.
To go to the beach, you need to cross a main road. Sea is rough and plenty of rocks, not suitable for swimming. There is no good shop in and around this hotel. Without your own transport is no fun staying here. airport is 30 to 40 by taxi costing about 800 baht.
Quality of food and breakfast is 3 stars not 5. The gymn is really basic and below par, better to go for a run instead of using the gymn. 
The Spa very good but after 20% the price is 1800 baht is still pricy.
reviews.positives: Nice big room and airy lobby, nice swimming pool.

reviews.title: Poor Location far away not directly on the beach
reviews.text: This is a package tourist hotel perfect for family. Good service but standard rooms. Far away from the main sites in Krabi and not directly on the beach. The beach there is not good but the pool is huge and excellent for families with children. Not great for single or couple travelers as not much to do unless you rent a car. Was greatly disappointed.
reviews.positives: Good quality price

reviews.title: Nice, clean hotel but not much to do around
reviews.text:
We stayed at the Sofitel in Krabi as part of our honeymoon in February.  The hotel's services were not as good and the staff not as kind as any of the places we stayed during our honeymoon (Phuket, Bali and Bangkok)  We did not have breakfast at the hotel as it did not look good.  

The main thing we didn't like about the hotel is that the closest town is 15 minutes away.  There's nothing to do around the hotel that is walking distance.  The hotel knows this so they have a shuttle to the closest town but it's not frequent enough and not free.  It really should be. . . We ended up taking hotel's shuttle (or a tuk tuk) to the town every night.  

They do offer a free boat tour to the Hong Island.  That's probably the best thing about this hotel and a must.  They do have a nice pool, a small beach front and many activities.
reviews.positives: Nice, clean rooms, many daily activities

reviews.title: Sofitel Phokeethra Krabi Resort & Spa
reviews.text:
The hotel is huge and sparsely build so you can have your privacy however the downside is you have to walk a long way if your rooms are right at the end.
The service is good. The hotel location is far , so good for honeymoons. If you want a piece of action Ao nang beach accomodation is a better choice.
reviews.positives: swimming pool is the best , it is huge !! spa is also good.
reviews.negatives: Restaurant is so - so and expensive. You can find better food at Ao nang at a cheaper pricing

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Hotel is really good but the rooms are not well maintained, improvement in the rooms is required.

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.text:
Horrible location, very far from everything, stuck in middle of No where, very hard to get a taxi and hotel shatel prices are ridiculous, very greedy hotel, charge triple for everything, i paid lot of money for 2 rooms for 6 ppl, had to raise my voice to get enough beds for everyone, after I confirmed with reservation that we will be 6 ppl,  
Never again, i will also not recommend.. 

Breakfast is good though, and pools are nice; but when you stuck in middle of no where you better have a good facility…
reviews.response:
Bonjour Aviram,

Sawasdee Kha! Warmest Thai greetings from Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf & Spa Resort. 

Thank you very much for staying with us during your recent trip to Krabi and for taking time to share your review on Agoda.com.

Your feedback about the location and the pricing is noted.  Please be advised that we are purposely located away from the busy tourist towns as we are a holiday resort and as a luxury hotel, we have many high operational costs to enable us to provide a five-star luxury experience for our guests in a safe and secure environment with trained staff in order to reach their expectations.

Once again, thank you for sharing your experience and we are glad that you enjoy the rest of your stay.

reviews.title: Similar to baking $600 for 7noght resort
reviews.text:
If so many things to do outside of resort like activities, just take cheaper one. 
Staff are obsessed to get tips or room is not cleaned professionally.
reviews.response:
Bonjour juhee,
Thank you very much for taking time to share your review on Agoda.com.

We hope that this experience will not impair your trust in Sofitel Krabi and we sincerely hope that you will give us another opportunity to better serve you again in the future.

reviews.room_type: Family Room, 1 King bed, Triple bunk beds with Private Balcony

reviews.title: Disappointed with stay
reviews.text: We arrived at the Sofitel with high expectations for good food and kind staff. The food was completely sub par! We've stayed at other Sofitel with fantastic food and this did not compare. Even their French pastries made by French chefs were not good. The staff seem overworked and were not helpful to customers. The servers in all the resterants seemed frazzled all the time, each one having too many tables. It always took 15 minutes or more to get even a drink at the restaurants. The pool was beautiful, but that was the only positive. We were in Thailand for two weeks and had fabulous service and food everywhere else we stayed. The Thai people are fantastic and helpful, however this hotel although in Thailand had very few Thai staff members. I would not recommend this hotel to anyone!
reviews.response: On behalf of Sofitel Krabi Phokeetra, please accept my deepest apologies for the inconvenience you faced during your stay with us regarding the staffs Your remark has been carefully noted and I can assure you the necessary steps have been taken to avoid such inconveniences in the future. Your feedback is extremely valuable to us as it helps us improve the quality of our service to better meet your needs. I hope we will have the pleasure of welcoming you again soon in Krabi.

reviews.title: Very Warm Service, Huge Pool and Good food
reviews.text: Very nice hotel but location not really strategic as the beach are not really accessible for swimmers etc. Nearest beach takes about 15mins taxi ride. Overall good experience but pricing slightly on the higher side considering low season and not all-inclusive package provided (ie no breakfast)
reviews.response: Dear MOHD, we appreciate your positive feedback about our hotel. We take pride in catering to our guests’ needs the best way we can. If you’re traveling to our area again soon, don’t hesitate to contact us.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives:
I recently stayed at the *Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf & Spa Resort* and had a very relaxing experience. Here's a breakdown of the positives and negatives:

Positives
- The atmosphere was quiet and gave off an old-money luxurious feel, perfect for unwinding.
- The hotel offers a wide range of activities, and the architecture, especially in the lobby, is absolutely stunning.
- The front office staff were excellent, with special mention to Talia from guest relations and a wonderful intern from Cambodia (whose name I unfortunately forgot), both of whom were incredibly helpful and attentive.
- The rooms were spacious and well-appointed. I stayed on the 1st floor with a pool view, while my sister’s room on the 2nd floor had a balcony facing the golf course.
- Meals were overall great, with dinner being the standout thanks to the delicious Thai cuisine.
- The pastries at breakfast were top-notch.
- The resort's location is peaceful, away from the noise of Ao Nang, but not completely isolated.

A special thanks to Talia for her help, including at a local 7/11, and for explaining the ongoing renovation process, which leaves me looking forward to returning after the updates.
reviews.negatives:
Negatives
- The hotel feels a bit outdated, particularly the bathrooms, where the water pressure was low and the shower head too small.
- The beach is not suitable for swimming, which might be disappointing for some.
- While dinner and breakfast pastries were great, breakfast options overall were limited, and room service wasn’t up to the standard of the restaurants.
- Restaurant dishes were a bit pricey and came in smaller portions compared to typical Thai servings.

reviews.title: nice room but far from everything, cant see sea
reviews.text: Relax atmosphere but crowded with French tourist groups. The location is far from everything and the hotel transportation is overpriced.
reviews.positives: room, swimming pool and staffs

reviews.title: Needs alot of improvement.
reviews.positives: - the number of resturants and the facilites
reviews.negatives:
- location was far but no shuttles to the city. They infact wanted to charge for shuttles to the city which I thought is a cheap move for a 5* hotel. And that to more expensive than a normal taxi.

- the hotel definetly requires renovation. Much much need rooms upgrade from room to furniture to corridors. And im not comparing it to dubai standards we had a 14 days stay at 5 hotels.

- breakfast options HAD to be increased. More like a 3-4* hotel to me at best compared to other hotels in thailand

reviews.title: clean a lot of choice in breakfast but very far
reviews.text: We appreciated staying at the hotel although it's very far and expensive to go out
reviews.positives: clean beautiful good services

reviews.title: Lots of family activities available
reviews.text: Krabi is a very beautiful place to visit. Sofitel is a good hotel but it is rather old. We went at high season so the staffs were extremely busy. room amenities were often incomplete after room cleaning. The hotel manager was extremely helpful, recommending us various options for dining venue (as hotel restaurants were fully booked).
reviews.positives: Sofitel has the biggest swimming pool in Th. There are lots of activities for families with young children at the hotel.

reviews.title: Not convenient
reviews.text: Had to top up 1500Thai Baht per night for connecting rooms upon arrival.  This was so as connecting rooms only available for superior room and luxury room.  We had booked 2 superior rooms from Agoda.  The only difference between the superior room and luxury room is an extra day bed.  Bad start to our holidays. If we had knew this, we would not have book Sofitel.
reviews.positives: Kids' club, gymnasium and breakfast spread

reviews.title: Relaxing, isolated, great pool, ugly beach
reviews.text: Our family of 3 (14 year old daughter) stayed at the Sofitel for 4 days.  The hotel was nice enough, quiet room, very large pool and quite decent restaurant.  Hotel per se was quite cheap for the quality (thanks to Agoda) and offer an executive 9-hole golf course.  We didnt play golf so I cant comment on the quality of the golf.  The hotel is quite remote from the main hub which is Ao Nong.  Therefore going to town for dinner or activities was quite expensive.  A 10 minute ride in-town was 500 baht or about US $15 a whapping $30 for 20 minute cab ride.  The hotel offered the transport to the city but it was more expensive than the local taxis.  Overall a decent experience, but if you go book your tours directly from the shops in Ao Nong rather than at the hotel which charge a steep premium for the service of making a phone call to book the tour operators!  Enjoy your travel!
reviews.positives: Clean and OK size room, superb pool and good restaurants

reviews.title: value for money but far from attractions
reviews.positives: Great Pool n pool bar

reviews.title: Comfortable & nice but far from central
reviews.text: The resort is very comfortable, staffs are very helpful & polite. It is very quiet & relax. It has very large swimming pool, pool side bar, rooms are relatively large with balcony. Ideal for those who want some peaceful & quiet life away from the cities. However, I would say it is located too far away from the central area. Unless you are planning to stay in the resort for all the time, it will be quite inconvenience & costly to travel to the central area for some local street food & shopping.
reviews.positives: Comfortable, quiet & relaxing

reviews.title: fairly
reviews.text: Hotel so may big  i have to walk very far. i prefer smaller hotel and close to
reviews.positives: staff / swiming pool /  nice room

reviews.title: Traditional Hotel with Huge Swimming Pool
reviews.text: We stayed one night at the Sofitel in Krabi on our way back to Bangkok from a tour of the southern Andaman islands. Its a beautiful, traditional hotel with all the gradeur you would expect from the Sofitel. The Swimming Pool is unbelievably huge (7000 m3) so if you like lazying by the pool and swimming all day... this is your address. Personally, we found it a little boring however after 2 weeks of island hopping and staying in various non-airco lodgings with little of no warm water, we were happy with the reliability of the Sofitel. The food at the pool bar was atrocious so we didn't try any of the other restaurants and went into Ao Nang for dinner. This would be a great hotel with kids and a hubby who likes to golf!
reviews.positives: Swimming Pool

reviews.title: nice & spacious
reviews.text: there's a great air of spaciousness and thus the air of relaxation within the hotel. The golf course also adds to this nice green quality.  Service wasn't the most attentive, except for the housekeeping staff who do fantastic job. We didn't mind that we're out of the main Ao Nang area for the peace and quiet.  The free speed boat trip to Hong Island is definitely worth doing.
reviews.positives: we had the luxury rooms, very spacious indeed
reviews.negatives: cleanliness of the pool, especially with its vast area, could be improved

reviews.title: B&B - Big and boring
reviews.text: I could not help it - I felt disappointed about the hotel considering the price of more than  As such, the hotel is great - at least the lobby and the pool. But everything else is big, impersonal and not particularly appealing. The corridors of the different wings are clean, generic and boring. Since you wander around quite a bit I would have appreciated some sort of local flair.
reviews.positives:
- Pool
- Sunset at pool
- Lobby area
reviews.negatives:
- Road close to the pool
- Shuttle bus is not free
- Check in done by trainee staff (not professional)
- Got a room allocated with view on parking lot.

reviews.title: Sofitel Krabi
reviews.text: We stayed in this hotel for six nights over Christmas.  We choose the Sofitel having stayed previously in the Sofitel in Hua Hin.  We were initially disappointed when we got to the room.  We were in a Luxury Ocean Room which I would not describe as luxury.  In my opinion it was no more than a standard room.  However, the rest of the hotel was great.  Fantastic staff, so friendly and helpful.  Top recommendations would be the free speedboat trip to Hong Island and the cookery class.  Overall we were happy with our stay but are unlikely to go back mainly because it was too large a hotel and the quality was not what we are used to.
reviews.positives: Great location on a lovely beach.  The shuttle speedboat to Hong Island is a great trip but you need to book up as it is busy.
reviews.negatives: Disappointing rooms.  We stayed in a Luxury Ocean Room and it was not better than a standard quality room.  It was certainly not luxury.  The Spa was expensive.

reviews.title: Break from the City
reviews.text:
Location of the hotel is far from the city crowd however for give us a break from the crowd is a good choice to stay. Overall the hotel facility is good big swimming pool, activity area, etc.

Hotel provides free boat service to Hong Island and you have to book it in advance. Kayak King and Windsurfing is provided free of charge by the hotel as well.

White Lotus restaurant in the hotel was a good choice of Thai food we were satisfied with its quality of foods and this is one of the best Thai food we had in Thailand.
reviews.positives: Design, Restaurant, Activity area are good
reviews.negatives: Slow service response

reviews.title: The 5-star resort is spacious but a bit old-fashioned. The pool condition is good, but the customer base is family-oriented. The staff attitude is good, but attention to detail seems necessary.
reviews.text: I stayed here because it was said to be the most famous in Krabi. The staff were friendly, and I was satisfied with the spacious swimming pool. However, since it is primarily targeted at families, I would not recommend it for couples. The pool water was also warm, making it nice to swim. There was a lifeguard, and the water was clean. (You have to pay for paddle bottles!) Overall, the resort was great, but there was one disappointing aspect. I think I booked without breakfast included. However, I thought breakfast was included, so when I mentioned my room number in front of the restaurant, they let me in. Later, they asked if I needed coffee and double-checked my room number. At that point, if they had confirmed that I was not included, they could have just told me... Instead, I ended up with a receipt for 4,000 baht for 4 people at 1,000 baht each... 😭 This isn’t the hotel’s fault, but typically they would inform you upon checking at the entrance, so it was a bit disappointing to receive the receipt without any explanation... It is my fault for not confirming, but I've been to so many 5-star hotels and resorts, and this has never happened before, so it was surprising! Haha. Due to the tour timing, I ended up eating only eggs, salad, and noodles in a hurry, and that 1,000 baht was a bit hard to take. 😅 Well, from the hotel’s perspective, it might look like I was eating without paying, but if it was not included, it seems like they should inform the customers! Haha. Also, the taxi cost is very expensive compared to Grab~~! If you need to catch your flight, be prepared since Grab is not easy to get!

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text: I booked as a solo traveler. The location is very remote, and you will have to pay for a shuttle if you'd like to go off the resort. There weren't many activities to do, and I felt as though the hotel tried to sponge money from their guests as they overcharge for every single thing - activities, transportation, even water and wifi. I've never stayed in a "luxury" hotel that had absolutely NO included amenities. I wouldn't choose to stay here again even if it was a romantic couples' vacation. The staff was pleasant but I could tell they made up their own prices in shuttle transportation as each time id ask for a price it would not coincidence with what the previous staff told me. Then when they nervously changed or dropped the price when I would question them. It just have a lot of mistrust with the hotel staff at this hotel. I even ordered a bottle of Perrier for breakfast and was charged $5 USD (which is twice as much as in the US). If I wanted to pay US prices in Thailand, I would've stayed in the US. I am regretful to have stayed here.

There are few thai restaurants 5 minutes walk from the hotel that serve better foods than the hotel restaurants.

reviews.title: Beautiful but for families
reviews.text: There is no denying this property has a beautiful pool however as a couple with no children this property felt like a family daycare. The adults only section of the pool wasn’t enforced, the pool bar constantly had kids sitting and yelling to each other and even the cocktail bar had kids sitting at the bar. The executive lounge is also very small and hidden away from the view so sunsets and missed. The the price it just wasn’t up to standard for me

reviews.title: A Satisfactory stay
reviews.text: Even though the Hotel had a handful of guests during our stay (evident from the crowd at the breakfast and in the pool and the large number of unlit rooms) we were given 2 rooms at one corner of the resort with no front view at all. The rather spacious balcony was a waste as we overlooked the boundary wall and the adjacent backyard of another dwelling. On requesting for a front (seafacing, pool or garden view) we were told rather politely they were reserved ! The food at the Cafe was very mediocre and not becoming of a top of the line Hotel. The only other dining option during the day was the poolside restaurant which served Western snacks and very few local dishes that too at lunch and dinner hours only. Room service was available though 24 hours.After our first lunch meal at the cafe, we did not eat in the Hotel for this reason other than the breakfast which was quite decent in choices.The boat trips in the Hotel speedboat were simply too exhorbitant charging for 1 person the amount in which a family of 4 could easily do a similar trip in a speedboat through local operators. The beach is across the road though but no facility of any Kayaking, beach chairs, etc provided or set up by the Hotel. Other than the swimming pool and the cleanliness, i would not go back for anything.

reviews.title: Secluded beach getaway with plenty of activities
reviews.text:
First off the bad stuff : no free wifi in rooms, only available at lobby area (which can get quite crowded in evenings as holiday-makers make plans for coming days) and warm + humid (early Aug). The fans in lobby area don't exactly blow at where people sit and lounge.

Now the good stuff:

1. Big awesome pool with bar where you literally sit in the pool and sip your drinks.
2. Secluded beach and quiet surroundings : Kluong Muong is far away from Ao Nang and the noisiness (could be a bad thing also as it will take about 299 THB / pax one-way hotel shuttle to get to Ao Nang, or 550 THB / one way taxi, 15-minutes).
3. Sofitel's famous So Spa : Very good spa (although I tried only their After-Sun treatment), big comfy rooms, excellent service, and of course L'Occitane spa materials
4. Attentive staff : Complained once to a manager-looking guy about our long wait for veal steak at the in-house Italian restaurant, got free cocktails from the bar after that  :)
5. Activities : Sofitel Krabi organizes plenty of exclusive hotel-only excursions to nearby islands. Prices are very reasonable and being limited to hotel guests means you don't have to crowd it out with other backpackers, or travel to Ao Nang to get your island fix.

Overall good place for people seeking a holiday "retreat".
reviews.positives: Attentive staff, big awesome pool, excellent So Spa

reviews.title: Good but not GREAT
reviews.text:
If you’ve been to some high end Sofitel resorts like in Bali, then this one will let you down. While rooms are spacious, they have that old feel. This includes the bathrooms too.

As for the property, it’s 15-20 mins from AaoNang beach, where the main action happens. Also it’s 45-50 mins from the airport.

The facilities are limited with not a really beach as part of the resort, but one where you cross the Main Street and go there. So in short, you only have a big pool that’s divided between kids and adults areas.

They have activities like yoga, Muay Thai, paddle tennis and more. Also a Pais excursion for sunrise and sunset tour. A few kids activities were there too.
2 restaurants and one All day dining along with one lounge and a gelato parlour is what’s onsite. Breaksfast has a decent spread, though I’ve seen Better at other Sofitels. The white Lotus is a nice restaurant with really tasty meal.

The gym is a sorry excuse and is very basic. Not what I expected from a Sofitel. 

Overall, for a *** A$ a night, it was not worth it. 

But I’ll deft recommend to anyone who wants to be away from the hustle and bustle of the touristy area and just wants to relax in a resort by the pool.

reviews.title: First stay at Sofitel
reviews.text:
I guess I was expecting a well run resort but there were a few disappointments that I want to alert others planning to stay here.

The pool is magnificently designed and lagoon like with children friendly floating slides etc., But how could anybody swim in a 28-30C water temperature? It’ s just downright uncomfortable cuz you feel like you are being cooked in the pool.

Secondly, the Maya restaurant closed from 4-6pm with last order at3:30p. After 4pm the a/c is turned off which means any guest who stays at the resort will have to order food to eat by the pool between 3:30-6pm. What about those who don’t want to eat outdoor? Room service is of course an option., but it is simply unacceptable for a resort of this caliber to not offer all day indoor dining facility. Especially for guests who eat a full breakfast and probably won’t want to eat or snack until after 3pm.

The wifi is definitely not up to snuff, there is no data flow even if your device shows all three bars of the wifi symbol. It’s deceiving so please keep this in mind.

The room cleaning service is another disappointment. Even after hanging the clean room sign at the door around 11am, nobody came to clean room till after 4pm. And we arrived back in the room at 4pm when we saw the cleaning staff open our room door to try to get inside to clean. We of course turned him away cuz we needed our privacy in the room. There was a 5 hour window for the staff to clean but apparently staff were too busy elsewhere cleaning or they simply didn’t even see the clean room sign. The sign is clearly useless.

reviews.title: Real Mixed Bag
reviews.text:
Great facilities for kids, outstanding pool area. Massive rooms for the price. Some of the best service I have ever experienced in Asia recommending food and wines for large groups with kids over a few days in both the Italian and Thai restaurants. And really good food on premise and great news is good range of restaurants within walking distance if that's what after you after. But...
Ridiculous baby sitting prices when the person doing the baby sitting isn't getting remotely what you are paying. Seriously sgd$100 for 3 hours to preside over your kids when they are asleep.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives:
This has been our third visit to the Sofitel Krabi. We discovered this fantastic hotel through a search for the longest warm-water pools in the world. Simply for the pool inaugurated by the princess, it is worth returning there.
Additionally, we are lovers of hotels with architectural and colonial interior design. We have become the most loyal customers of the Indian restaurant, where we established a very friendly relationship with Chef Mohabir, Restaurant Manager Ms. Kuhn Sao, and the team members Ms. Pahn, Ankit, Janak, Jib, Jan, and Maj, who served us with unparalleled professionalism and kindness, delighting our palates at every dinner.
Feeling at ease is generated through the five senses:
	•	Sight: The hotel offers much beauty to behold.
	•	Smell: The plants and deep cleanliness delighted our sense of smell.
	•	Taste: The chef Mohabir’s specialties are exceptional.
	•	Touch: The skeletal-muscular system is cared for by professionals in one of Thailand’s best spas, where soothing music and skilled hands induce peace of spirit.
However, unfortunately, as I mentioned and criticized in my text from last year, it is precisely the sense of hearing that suffers the most—indeed, it is mortified in this hotel. This compels me to refrain from giving the hotel the maximum stars in my rating. I propose an analysis by management regarding this matter. Once we leave the spa, speakers placed in the lobby or garden at various times fill the air with jarring sounds dominated by bass components, either to celebrate or to accompany social activities for children by the pool. I consider the high volume harmful to children’s eardrums and unbearable for mature guests like us who come to relax.
In summary, a formidable vacation hotel experience!
Best regards,
Kuhn Egon

reviews.title: Very luxurious resort hotel
reviews.text:
My family stayed in this hotel for three nights and four days. Unexpectedly we realised that this hotel is very luxurious and upscale, considering the cost we paid. Hotel was very quiet and peaceful. Staff services were also very good. One of the best things about this hotel is breakfast. In particular, bakery section was excellent! The swimming pool was gigantic and clean. If you want to stay in hotel all day, then this is a good choice for your family (one suggestion to the hotel: it might be better if you have beach balls in swimming pool area).

One minor thing is location. Beach nearby is not good for swimming so you have to go to Ao Nang beach by taxi or hotel shuttle, which are very costly. There are few restaurants or marts nearby. If you have maps or GPS, renting a car is much cheaper.
reviews.positives: Hotel facility, swimming pool, staff

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Nice pool and kids loved it. Breakfast was great.  Restaurant choices decent.  Staff are nice but do need more during peak hours.
reviews.negatives: We knew it was not exactly a beach resort but activity center can organize tours in land or at sea.  Hotel has character and hence feels a bit tiered.

reviews.title: Nice hotel rooms with a wonderful swimming pool
reviews.text:
The hotel is classic and classy, a bit old school but really well maintained. The room was huge and really clean, there is absolutely nothing negative to say about it a part from the TV which was 10 years old, but honestly who cares about the TV in such a tropical paradise.
The hotel staff was really well trained. They attended our few requests really fast. Another thing to underline, they were human unlike certain other hotel chains. Not the kind of staff who are reciting their text by memory like robots, they were truly interacting with the customers by having genuine conversation. It was pleasant to experience.
In terms of food, we really enjoyed the breakfast which provides a wide choice of food. Pancakes amongst other delicacies were delicious. We found that the bar/restaurant at the pool was too pricy, we found that 1000bahts for 2 sandwiches is a bit too much in Thailand especially when the taste had nothing special.

For the negative side, we found the hotel really noisy, because it is facing a road where many trucks are passing by. There were also work inside the hotel on Saturday. Even on the swimming pool the filters were so noisy, so finally it was difficult to be relaxed at 100%.

We were also expecting a private beach and we were quite disappointed. The beach is definitely not a place where you can lay down and tan. For the one who never went to Krabi before, don't go there for the beaches you will be disappointed especially compared to other places in Thailand. In addition to that be prepared to open your purse. From the hotel to Aonang will become costly on a daily basis (only few restaurants around).

So this place is only recommended for people (families with child I guess) who want to be in a secluded area with nothing much around. But for young couples in their 30's I guess there surely are better places to stay. So even if there were few positive aspects about this hotel, we will probably choose another one if we ever come back to Krabi.
reviews.positives: Spacious room, wonderful swimming pool, delicious breakfast

reviews.positives: The Sofitel's service and facilities are probably the best in Krabi.  The staff are extremely professional and went well out of their way to ensure that we had a pleasant stay.  The pool is definitely the property's most impressive facility.  According to their marketing, it is the largest free-from pool in Thailand (or maybe just Krabi), but it is quite impressive, and the pool-side bar service was extremely attentive.  The club lounge is also a pleasant facility and made our stay even more enjoyable.
reviews.negatives: The main thing to note about the Sofitel is that while it is technically "beachfront", getting to the beach requires walking across the lawn and crossing a road to what amounts to a not particularly impressive beach.  This is why the pool is the main attraction at this property.  It's also why you may not want to bother with an ocean view room.  The modest traffic on the road wasn't noisy during our stay, but it also doesn't provide quite the picturesque view that you might expect.

reviews.title: Many activities, huge pool and nice for families
reviews.text:
We liked our stay in this resort because of it's very nice appearance and the many activities (windsurfing, sailing, golfing, thai boxing, bicycling, table tennis, tennis, running, kicker, gym, sauna, swimming, yoga, tai chi, ...) that were offered - mostly complimentary or at quite a reasonable price. The very huge pool is the biggest in Thailand one of the biggest in south east Asia. The beach made quite a mixed impression: on high tide, there won't be any beach at all, on very low tide the water begins some 50-100m behind the black rocks and on medium tide the water covers the rocks leaving a very nice, wide and sandy beach. The nearby island can be reached by the complimentary speed boat trip.
Unfortunately, WiFi (176 THB/30 minutes) as well as food and drinks are very expensive. The bed was much too hard, the A/C too strong and in an 5* hotel the TV should be a big LCD/LED not a small ray tube TV.
This resort is perfect for families, because of the many activities - also some of them special for kids.

We had a great stay here. Its a lovely but old hotel, hence not quite up to its 5 star rating at the moment.
The staff and service was great when we were there, the breakfast buffet was awesome - you can tell the French influence and the lady who gave the cooking class (Yala) was fantastic.
The kids club staff were great too.
Downsides were the big mine/ plant just down the road and the resulting stream of lorries going up and down, the shuttle into town was more expensive then local taxis - no brainer to fix that one... and the beach isn't great. Fun for crab hunting at night though.
Some past reviews suggested the food wasn't great but all our meals were really good and/ or its a only a short walk or taxi ride down the road to find lots of other cheaper local food places.

reviews.title: Great experience for family stay
reviews.text: The trip was very relaxing. With young children, my family choose to hang around the resort by the beach and pool-side majority of the time. Breakfast food is great with a wide variety of choices to pick from. Room is spacious and comfortable. Rooms are clean but can be slightly better. (strands of hair found on floor under the bed). Staff are all very courteous aside from one uncharacteristic rude encounter at the ice-cream shop (The staff may have been overworked and tired at the end of the day, giving her the benefit of the doubt). The children's play area had plenty of activities for kids. There is also a friendly staff to watch over the children. Overall, justifiable as a 5-star resort given the overall experience. Would recommend this place to families with young children.

The area is away from shops so take note that if there is a downpour in the afternoon you might be left stranded in the hotel with not much food options as the restaurants open only for dinner at 6pm. Only a cafe sells a tiny selection of simple sandwiches. So do keep some snacks stocked.

Staff was very attentive and helpful. A bit like Club Med, the resort provides sufficient activities to keep you occupied. Nice to catch the sunset at the resort's beach. Overall a good stay and would return.

reviews.title: relaxing for adult enjoyable for kids
reviews.text:
We had a very pleasant stay. It was an all girls holiday. 2 mums and 3 little girls. Check in was very smooth.  The kids were also welcomed, greeted and had their own "check in"by the staff. You could see that the girls felt really special. When the rooms were ready, staff personally showed us to the room. Explained and pointed us is the general direction of the facilities in the hotel. And when it came to showing us where the kids club was, they specifically spoke to the kids and gave them the instuctions on how to get there. As a mother, I felt very comfortable leaving the girls at the kids club. The staff there are really child friendly people. Although I personally think it could do with a little refurbishment on the inside.
Breakfast had a good spread but we found that lunch was a problem. Menu at the poolside was limited. Other restaurants closed except the coffee house which only served sandwiches drinks and desserts. No biggy there!
We had a few hiccups at the hotel. There was a problem with the pool pump. So water was murky and sandy. On the second last day we didn't have water in the rooms. But the problem was rectified quickly. On the whole, I had a very relaxing stay. The kids loved it there. We never felt the need to venture out of the hotel. The activities desk arranged an elephant ride and a visit to an island. Which all turned out perfectly. The selling point of this hotel apart from the pool and lovely grounds it sits on is the staff. They go the extra mile to make your day just right. My heartfelt thanks to all staff.....reception,  activities desk,  kids club, dining,  housekeeping and just everyone. The kids are looking froward to seeing you again.
reviews.positives: attentive staff, great selection for breakfast and the atmosphere in the hotel was perfect for for all ages

reviews.title: Better Staff training could make it excellent
reviews.text:
Very nice property..one of the few that I would rate as ideal for guests from a cross-section - honeymooners as well as families with kids. The big expansive pool is nicely landscaped with hammocks hanging invitingly across the lawns surrounding the pool. Breakfast lounge menu gives you nothing to complain about. They have a nice coffee shop , besides an Indian/Thai restaurant and of course the French restaurant and a bar. There's also Reading room, Kid's centre , Gym, Tennis court and a small golf course.

Rooms are spacious and well appointed - ask for 1st or 2nd floor rooms if you need sea views.
Krabi has lots of outdoor options and the activity centre offers in house booking..of course you pay a premium against street prices for the same tours/activities.

What could be better ? - we had a couple of frustrations with the house keeping service. as well as front desk....all staff are extremely polite and always smiling and cheerful ..the issue is around their ability to clearly understand requests and instructions in English (understandably though)..sometimes even basic questions or requests are met with a smiling but completely blank expression and you know you are in for a prolonged attempt to articulate what you need...we actually gave up on a couple of occasions :)-

reviews.positives: The breakfast is a buffet with a large range of options. The pool is the largest in Thailand. Room was large and comfy. Restaurants were good - Italian and Asian. Nice spa. Beach access across the street. Good excursions - we went to Hong Island.
reviews.negatives: This isn’t a negative, just an observation. As a couple who enjoyed the privacy and ambiance at Rayavadee, the Sofitel is a larger resort hotel that caters more to families and golfers.

reviews.title: Beyond expectation
reviews.text:
we had a pleasant stay at sofitel and it was definitely beyond our expectation. the hotel is huge and beautiful and the staff are really friendly and helpful, the kind that would go the extra mile to make your stay a pleasant one. 

the swimming pool is big and long! not to forget, it is slightly heated..how awesome is that? we spent most of our time at the pool, swimming and sometimes just sitting at the pool bench, waiting for sunset. the view is fantastic as it right opposite the beach.

the hotel offers a lot of packages at very reasonable prices. for instance, we took the hong island package which costs us around per person and we got a return transfer by sofitel's luxury speedboat, beach towel, beach mat, a butler who will be there to help you in case you need any assistance, lunch and drinks, and also bread for the fishes! definitely worth every penny spent.

if you are on your honeymoon or anniversary vacation, be sure to tell the staff. we got a complimentary happy honeymoon cake during our stay :) 

the only downside is there is no free wi-fi or internet access. you have to pay thb100per hour or thb235 per day to access the internet. i think this is a bit inconvenient as i feel handicapped without internet!!
reviews.positives: Friendly and helpful staff, awesome swimming pool

reviews.title: Excellent Stay, highlly recoomended for families
reviews.positives:
They have a huge pool with areas dedicated for children and adults as well. The pool bar is also nice and serves finger food  as well. The facilities are fantastic with golf course inside the report only. 
If i go again to Krabi, ill stay again.
reviews.negatives: It a bit away from the main city thus no hustle and bustle; but i didnt mind as massage parlors, grocery and few restaurants are close by. Breakfast can improve in terms of food taste.

reviews.title: fabulous resort
reviews.text:
Sofitel was fantastic.

Krabi's beaches are some of the best to be found anywhere and lie within easy access from the main town of Krabi. 

Each has its own attractions to offer the tourist -- be it simple relaxation in the sun and sea, seclusion, or the opportunity to indulge in water sports and other activities in the ultimate tropical paradise.

definitely recommend it
reviews.positives: pool

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives:
We loved our stay , very family friendly hotels with lots of activities for kids.

A lot of excursions available to be planned through the hotel.

Super friendly staff , and very clean & comfortable hotel.

reviews.title: Lovely and very friendly staff around to help out
reviews.text: the problem is, i got food poisioning on the last day of the trip. i ordered stir fry vege that came with mushroom,for my last breakfast, maybe the mushroom is not clean properly till now im still sick and been out of work for 3 days already. please take note of this.
reviews.positives: the view is nice

reviews.title: I really enjoyed.
reviews.positives: The staff, the restaurants the facilities are amazing.  It really is a wonderful resort.
reviews.negatives: The beds need an upgrade.  The doors are designed to slam on there own or if you try to close slow they still slam, it’s possible to close quietly but that involves your key card which nobody does, long story short, you hear a lot of slamming doors that night before bed and in the morning as people are going to breakfast.

reviews.title: nice classy hotel, good friendly service
reviews.text:
nice big rooms, very comfortable to say the least.  there's a nice big pool if you care to go for a dip or a swim.  

the location is quite far from town, works well if you just like to stay in and enjoy the place.  on the other hand, there's free shuttle to aonang beach town.

gym is small though.
reviews.positives: best memories were the friendly staffs and service from sofitel

reviews.title: Amazing for a weekend getaway!
reviews.positives: The hotel is very luxurious. It's so spacious and the pool is one of a kind. The staff are so kind and friendly, and the bar in the evening with live music is great fun. The location is great for a quiet and relaxing getaway, with the beach quite literally a few steps away. The breakfast was amazing! Such a variety! There are lots of places to eat if you walk down the road, so you're not subjected to only eating at the hotel for breakfast, lunch and dinner.
reviews.negatives: The hotel is a little outdated, it could do with some paint in areas. The pool water was almost too hot (but this is not a fault of the hotel), we just happened to be there during a heatwave, but might want to bear it in mind.

reviews.title: Excellent hotel
reviews.text: The hotel has a huge swimming pool in a beautiful garden. However since it is quite new, there is little shade as the trees are still young. The beach is unsuitable for swimming. There are hardly any chairs and no umbrellas on the beach. Whereas the beach itself is sandy, once entering there are plenty of (sharp) stones in the water. The location is somehow far from where action takes place, so you are more or less dependent on the facilities of the hotel.
reviews.positives: Rooms, breakfast is excellent

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: The pool is very nice. Breakfast was great as well.

Great Hotel.  Room and Facility.

But the hotel should provide transport to near by food outlets and restaurants...

Room

terrible service at dinner

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: The best swimming pool I have seen with loads of activities. Very good resort

reviews.title: Super GRAND!!!
reviews.text: I was very impressed with the immense hotel grounds and long drive way to get to the open hotel lobby, which had huge doorways, high ceilings and was one of the few places in Thailand, never mind South East Asia that gave that Grand, Executive feeling that will make you feel underdressed in flip flops, shorts and a T-shirt. Because of the sheer size of the hotel, with hundreds of rooms, the walk to our room seemed to take a while but it was well worth it when we got to our room which had a good view of the pool and the ocean. The room was very large, with a very traditional, colonial feel to it. It didn’t take us long to get into our swimming wear and head down to the pool which was by far the biggest I’ve ever seen in my life at a whopping 16,000 sq m!!! It seemed to go on for ever. What I particularly liked was the food we ordered from the bar. Yes the prices were Western standard but well worth it. The Roast Beef sandwich and tropical shake went down very well sitting on the stools, half submerged in the pool. After a very relaxing day in the pool, we just crossed the road, walked along the beach and drove into tow in our rented car later in the evening for some dinner. This is the perfect place to stay on the last day of your trip in Krabi if you’re tired of activities and just want to spend your time relaxing by the pool or on the beach before catching the taxi to the airport.
reviews.positives:
- Ridiculously large pool! 
- Great pool side bar menu!
- Beautiful large grounds
- quiet location
- A hotel that can boast 5 stars for its executive feel.
- Large, comfortable rooms.
- Close to beach
reviews.negatives:
- Prices are a bit steep for Thailand. Breakfast buffet in particular!
- Located in Klong Muang, so it is a bit far from town, hence pricey taxi rides. Good if you prefer to be away from Ao Nang and in isolation.
- A road in between the beach and hotel grounds with trucks driving by now and then.

reviews.title: Enjoyed our stay

reviews.negatives: The beach is dissappointing, but seems similar to other beaches around, so take the boat tours.. because of the tides sometimes you can not even walk on the beach

There are some restaurants nearby and a 7-11. Good location to wind down for a couple of days.
reviews.negatives: It was a bit too big for my choice. Good for families.

reviews.title: beautiful place in krabi
reviews.text: the place is fabulous for family , great breakfast , and 2 great restaurant , combine in one as indian and thai food . the hotel is great BUT a bit of lack of lucidity , we were a family , 2 very young kids , in very low season and still they manage to put us at the end of the corner of the hotel ... so we do have to walk a lot to get back to the room , apart of that , it is worth it IF u want to stay IN . OUTSIDE there is NOTHING unless you go 2 km further to find some nice street food

reviews.title: great fusion of  traditional french and thai

we chose this hotel as we thought they still offer free trip to koh hong, which was actually terminated and charged around 1800thb up/person.
but anyway we liked the hotel very much as decor and atmosphere were beautiful, their facilities incl huge swimming pool and well equipped kids club were very nice and all staffs were smart and friendly.
only thing they can improve is variety at buffet. food was not that good to meet the price.

reviews.title: Lovely pool vacation
reviews.text: It was so beautiful and the pool was the best and biggest ive ever seen! The staff were ever so friendly and made sure we were made to feel very welcome. They decorated my sister's room as she was there with her husband on their honeymoon. As tag-alongs, my mum and I also had chocolates in our rooms. beautiful place.

Will be back.

Relaxing. The delay in serving food in one of the dinner was compensated with a genuine apology and good care of us on our next visit.

But if want to go to ao nang, krabi town night market and airport is a bit far. In conclusion i still like the Sofitel Luxury Hotel quite much.
